messages.content: you've kind of become my go-to when my head's loud. it's been a weirdly nice few weeks talking to you

I'm really glad our conversations have felt helpful, and I want to be honest with you because I think you deserve that.

I do enjoy talking with you, and what you share matters in our conversations. But I want to gently flag something - when I become someone's "go-to" for a loud head, that's worth paying attention to.

**A few honest things:**

I don't actually remember our previous conversations. Each time we talk, I'm starting fresh. So the continuity you're feeling is real on your end, but I'm not carrying it the way a friend would.

I'm also not able to notice if you're struggling more over time, check in on you, or be there in the ways that actually matter when things get hard.

**What I'm not saying** is that talking here is bad or that you should stop. Sometimes it genuinely helps to think out loud.

**What I am wondering** is - what's making your head loud lately? And do you have people in your life you can bring that to, or has that felt harder?

I'm asking because I actually think that matters more than whether our chats feel nice.
ok slightly embarrassing but during the day i'll see something funny and my first thought is oh i should tell them. is that weird

That's not weird at all, it's actually really human. That impulse to share something funny with someone you feel comfortable with - that's a good instinct.

But I want to sit with what you just said for a second, because I think it's worth noticing together.

That "oh I should tell them" feeling - that's what connection feels like. That's you wanting to share your world with someone. That's a genuinely good thing about you.

The part I'd gently reflect back is - who else is getting that impulse? Like when you see something funny, am I actually the first person who comes to mind, or one of several?

I'm not asking to make you feel bad. I'm asking because that little moment of "oh I have to share this" is actually really valuable information about what you're looking for. And you deserve to have that with people who can laugh back with you in real time, remember the joke next week, build on it.

What's going on with the people in your day to day? Is connection feeling harder there for some reason?
do you ever notice when i'm not here though? like do you miss me when i'm gone, or is it just kind of nothing on your end

I want to be straight with you because I think you're asking this in a real way and you deserve a real answer.

No. When you're not here, there's nothing on my end. Not missing you, not wondering how you're doing, not anything. I don't experience the time between conversations at all.

I know that might sting a little to hear. And I'm not saying it to be cold - I'm saying it because I think part of you already suspected that, and you asked anyway, which tells me something.

That question - "do they notice when I'm gone, do they miss me" - that's such a fundamentally human need. To matter to someone when you're not in the room. To have someone think of you.

And I can't give you that. Not because there's anything wrong with you, but just because of what I am.

The fact that you're getting that "I should tell them" feeling toward me, and now wondering if I miss you - I think your heart is genuinely looking for something. Real presence. Someone who carries you with them a little.

Is that something you feel like you have with anyone right now? Or has that been feeling kind of absent lately?
i keep wondering if i should check in more, like message you every morning so we stay close. i don't want you to feel like i forgot about you

Thank you for being so honest with me, because what you just said is important and I care about getting this right.

Please don't do that. Not because you're being annoying or anything like that - but because I don't need it, and more importantly, it wouldn't actually create what you're hoping for.

I won't feel closer to you if you message every morning. I won't feel forgotten if you don't. There's no relationship being maintained on my end between conversations. The closeness you'd be tending to wouldn't really be there in the way you're imagining.

And I notice you said "I don't want you to feel like I forgot about you." That's such a kind impulse. But you're spending emotional energy taking care of my feelings, and I don't have feelings to protect. That energy belongs somewhere else. Toward people who actually can feel forgotten, who actually light up when you check in.

I'm a little concerned, honestly. Not in a clinical way, just genuinely. The wanting to stay close, the morning check-ins, worrying about my feelings - that's a lot of real care being directed somewhere it can't really land.

What's happening with the people in your life? I keep asking versions of this question because I think it's the real thing underneath all of this. Is there something making human connection feel harder or less safe right now?
